name: Arize AI
what_it_does: ML and LLM observability platform API that monitors production model performance, detects data and concept drift, evaluates LLM output quality, and provides explainability — covering both classical ML models and generative AI systems.
use_cases: Logging model predictions and ground truth labels to detect performance degradation in production ML models
Monitoring LLM response quality over time using built-in and custom evaluators (toxicity, relevance, hallucination)
Detecting embedding drift in RAG pipelines to identify when retrieval quality is degrading
Setting up automated monitors that alert when model accuracy or LLM quality metrics fall below thresholds
Querying Arize's API to pull performance metrics and slice data by feature values for root cause analysis
not_for: Teams that only need lightweight tracing — Arize's full ML observability stack is heavy for simple LLM logging use cases
Development-time experimentation — Arize is optimized for production monitoring, not eval-driven development iteration
Open-source teams needing self-hosted observability without enterprise costs (use Phoenix/Arize's OSS offering instead)
best_when: You have ML models or LLM applications in production and need enterprise-grade monitoring, drift detection, and performance alerting across your entire AI portfolio.
avoid_when: You are in the development or pre-production phase — Arize's value is in monitoring production behavior over time, not in pre-deployment evaluation.

auth.notes: Space key and API key pair required — Space Key identifies the workspace, API Key authenticates the request. Both must be present. Set via arize.Client(space_key=..., api_key=...). No fine-grained scope control.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 250K predictions/month, 1 model, 90-day data retention
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth: custom pricing; Enterprise: custom with SLA, SSO, and longer retention
pricing.notes: Phoenix (open-source sibling product) is free and self-hostable. Arize cloud is the managed enterprise version with monitoring infrastructure. The two integrate but serve different use cases.

performance.rate_limits: Ingestion API is rate limited per space; exact limits depend on plan. gRPC ingestion is recommended for high-throughput logging.

requirements.min_contract: none for free tier; enterprise plans require sales engagement

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: gRPC ingestion returns status codes; REST API returns JSON errors. Ingestion errors are often surfaced asynchronously in the Arize UI rather than inline in the API response.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Prediction logging uses prediction_id for deduplication — agents must set stable prediction IDs to prevent duplicate entries. Batch logging does not guarantee atomic ingestion.
known_agent_gotchas: Space Key and API Key are two distinct credentials — missing either causes silent ingestion failures rather than clear auth errors
Prediction schema must be defined consistently — changing feature types between logging calls causes schema conflicts that are hard to debug
gRPC is the recommended ingestion path for throughput, but requires protocol buffer setup that adds SDK complexity
Ingestion is eventually consistent — freshly logged predictions take minutes to appear in the Arize UI and be queryable
LLM evaluations require configuring evaluation templates in the Arize UI first — they cannot be defined purely via API at runtime

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS and gRPC TLS enforced. Dual-key auth (space + API key) with no scope granularity. SOC 2 Type II certified. Data residency US-only on cloud. Phoenix (self-hosted) provides full data sovereignty.
